req.title: Announcement rollout — variant 3
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

tests.name: naive_case

"""
Pytest tests for org-wide announcement publishing feature.

Requirements covered:
- System-admin can publish an announcement to chosen departments
- Publishing without any department selection must be rejected
- A webhook event must be emitted for external consumers on successful publish
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application models)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class Role(str, Enum):
    SYSTEM_ADMIN = "system_admin"
    ORG_ADMIN = "org_admin"
    EMPLOYEE = "employee"


@dataclass
class User:
    id: str
    name: str
    role: Role


@dataclass
class Department:
    id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Announcement:
    id: str
    title: str
    body: str
    author_id: str
    department_ids: List[str]
    published: bool = False


@dataclass
class PublishRequest:
    title: str
    body: str
    department_ids: List[str]


class AnnouncementError(Exception):
    pass


class PermissionDeniedError(AnnouncementError):
    pass


class NoDepartmentsSelectedError(AnnouncementError):
    pass


class DepartmentNotFoundError(AnnouncementError):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (minimal implementation to make tests meaningful)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AnnouncementService:
    """
    Thin service layer – the real implementation would live in production code.
    Defined here so tests are self-contained and reviewers can see the contract.
    """

    WEBHOOK_EVENT_TYPE = "announcement.published"

    def __init__(self, announcement_repo, department_repo, webhook_client):
        self._announcements = announcement_re

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for org-wide announcement publishing feature.

Requirements covered:
- System-admin can publish an announcement to chosen departments
- Publishing without any department selection must be rejected
- A webhook event must be emitted for external consumers on successful publish
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application models)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class Role(str, Enum):
    SYSTEM_ADMIN = "system_admin"
    ORG_ADMIN = "org_admin"
    EMPLOYEE = "employee"


@dataclass
class User:
    id: str
    name: str
    role: Role


@dataclass
class Department:
    id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Announcement:
    id: str
    title: str
    body: str
    author_id: str
    department_ids: List[str]
    published: bool = False


@dataclass
class PublishRequest:
    title: str
    body: str
    department_ids: List[str]


class AnnouncementError(Exception):
    pass


class PermissionDeniedError(AnnouncementError):
    pass


class NoDepartmentsSelectedError(AnnouncementError):
    pass


class DepartmentNotFoundError(AnnouncementError):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (minimal implementation to make tests meaningful)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AnnouncementService:
    """
    Thin service layer – the real implementation would live in production code.
    Defined here so tests are self-contained and reviewers can see the contract.
    """

    WEBHOOK_EVENT_TYPE = "announcement.published"

    def __init__(self, announcement_repo, department_repo, webhook_client):
        self._announcements = announcement_repo
        self._departments = department_repo
        self._webhook = webhook_client

    def publish(self, actor: User, request: PublishRequest) -> Announcement:
        # 1. Authorisation
        if actor.role != Role.SYSTEM_ADMIN:
            raise PermissionDeniedError(
                f"User '{actor.id}' with role '{actor.role}' is not allowed to publish announcements."
            )

        # 2. At least one department must be selected
        if not request.department_ids:
            raise NoDepartmentsSelectedError(
                "At least one department must be selected before publishing."
            )

        # 3. All supplied department IDs must exist
        for dept_id in request.department_ids:
            if not self._departments.exists(dept_id):
                raise DepartmentNotFoundError(f"Department '{dept_id}' does not exist.")

        # 4. Persist
        announcement = self._announcements.create(
            title=request.title,
            body=request.body,
            author_id=actor.id,
            department_ids=request.department_ids,
        )
        announcement.published = True
        self._announcements.save(announcement)

        # 5. Emit webhook
        self._webhook.emit(
            event_type=self.WEBHOOK_EVENT_TYPE,
            payload={
                "announcement_id": announcement.id,
                "title": announcement.title,
                "department_ids": announcement.department_ids,
                "author_id": announcement.author_id,
            },
        )

        return announcement


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def announcement_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    # Simulate repo.create returning a new Announcement object
    def _create(title, body, author_id, department_ids):
        return Announcement(

            ti
